message:
chore: update beads issue tracker and Cargo.lock

- issues.jsonl: mark bd-mblr.6.7 as in_progress (critical-path no-mock
  unit assertions now underway)
- Cargo.lock: regenerated to reflect new harness dependencies
  (fsqlite-ast, fsqlite-parser, fsqlite-vdbe)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-mblr.6.4","title":"Unit fixture corpus and deterministic seed harness","description":"Create reusable deterministic unit-test fixtures/seeds for storage pages, SQL statements, and execution traces so failures are exactly reproducible. Output: fixture catalog + seed contract consumed across crates.","design":"Create shared fixture APIs/utilities with explicit normalization rules for deterministic comparisons.","acceptance_criteria":"- Deterministic fixture corpus exists for storage pages, SQL statements, and execution traces.
- Seed contract defines how fixtures are selected and replayed across crates.
- Fixtures are versioned with compatibility notes to prevent silent semantic drift.","notes":"Fixture reuse should reduce duplicated setup logic and improve cross-crate failure comparability.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:33.540618686Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T05:55:38.367865663Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T05:55:38.367841318Z","close_reason":"Implemented: unit_fixtures.rs with FixtureSeed contract, 49 fixtures (8 page, 22 SQL, 14 value, 5 trace), FixtureCatalog API, 27 tests all passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:33.540618686Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:51.826778155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1434,"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-mblr.6.4 Completion — Unit Fixture Corpus and Deterministic Seed Harness

### Agent: BrownMouse (Session 23)

### Deliverables

1. **New module**: `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/unit_fixtures.rs` (~1,500 lines)
2. **Registered** in `lib.rs` as `pub mod unit_fixtures`

### Seed Contract

- `FIXTURE_SEED_BASE = 0x4649_5854_5552_4500` (hex "FIXTURE")
- `FixtureSeed::derive(scope_id)` — standard entry point for tests
- `FixtureSeed::derive_with(base, domain_tag, scope_id)` — explicit domain isolation
- `FixtureSeed::child(index)` — derive sub-fixtures from parent seed
- Domain tags: `page`, `sql`, `trace`, `value` — no cross-domain collisions
- Derivation: `xxh3_64(base_seed_bytes || domain_tag || scope_id)`

### Fixture Categories

| Category | Count | Coverage |
|----------|-------|----------|
| Page     | 8     | All 4 page types (LeafTable, InteriorTable, LeafIndex, InteriorIndex), boundary sizes (512, 4096, 65536), single/multi-cell variants |
| SQL      | 22    | All 8 taxonomy families (SQL, TXN, FUN, VDB, PLN, PGM, EXT, CLI) |
| Value    | 14    | All 5 storage classes (NULL, INTEGER, REAL, TEXT, BLOB) with boundary values |
| Trace    | 5     | Key VDBE opcode sequences (SELECT const, table scan, INSERT, DELETE, aggregate) |
| **Total**| **49**| Full coverage of fixture categories |

### Key Types

- `FixtureSeed` — deterministic seed with derivation contract
- `PageFixture` — storage page with raw data and metadata
- `SqlFixture` — categorized SQL statements with behavior tags
- `ValueFixture` — storage class value representations
- `TraceFixture` — VDBE opcode sequence expectations
- `FixtureCatalog` — unified entry point (build, filter, validate, serialize)

### Tests (27 total, all passing)

- Seed contract: determinism, scope isolation, domain isolation, child derivation
- Page fixtures: determinism, valid headers, cell counts, type coverage, boundary sizes
- SQL fixtures: determinism, family coverage, non-empty statements, behavior tags
- Value fixtures: determinism, storage class coverage
- Trace fixtures: determinism, valid opcodes
- Catalog: determinism, unique IDs, JSON roundtrip, summary stats, filtering

### Quality Gates

- `cargo check -p fsqlite-harness`: PASS
- `cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib unit_fixtures`: 27/27 PASS
- `cargo fmt`: clean
","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:55:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.6.5","title":"CI unit-test shard matrix and summary artifacts","description":"Run unit suites in CI shard matrix by crate family with deterministic ordering and publish concise per-shard failure summaries plus raw logs. Output: reliable CI unit-test workflow with triage-ready artifacts.","design":"Use manifest-driven shard composition to keep CI config maintainable and reproducible locally.","acceptance_criteria":"- CI executes unit suites in deterministic shard matrix by crate family or risk tier.
- Each shard publishes concise summary + raw logs/artifacts for failing tests.
- Shard strategy balances runtime and signal without hiding flaky behavior.","notes":"CI unit matrix should align naming and artifact conventions with E2E gates for operator consistency, but it should not be blocked by final flake-policy tuning so implementation can proceed in parallel.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:33.582757149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:10:14.843547916Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:33.582757149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:55.587175625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:55.937532522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:56.237186092Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.5","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:56.522450512Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.6.6","title":"Unit failure diagnostics contract and helpers","description":"Standardize rich failure output for unit tests (invariant ID, seed/fixture ID, state snapshot, diff hints) with shared helpers/macros where appropriate. Output: diagnostics contract and adoption checklist.","design":"Provide lightweight helper APIs that standardize metadata capture while preserving crate-specific context details.","acceptance_criteria":"- Diagnostics contract defines required unit failure fields: invariant_id, seed/fixture_id, state snapshot pointers, and diff hints.
- Shared helpers/macros are adopted by target crates without reducing assertion clarity.
- Contract compliance is tested so regressions in failure quality are caught automatically.","notes":"Aim for fast root-cause localization from a single CI failure log. Unit-diagnostics work should proceed in parallel with E2E schema work, then harmonize field conventions during cross-track conformance review.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:33.966923035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:03:13.425812718Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:03:13.425793903Z","close_reason":"Implemented: test_diagnostics.rs with DiagContext, DiagReport, 3 macros, diff/hex/snapshot helpers, 8-item adoption checklist, 30 tests all passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:33.966923035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.6","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:52.098562698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1435,"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-mblr.6.6 Completion — Unit Failure Diagnostics Contract and Helpers

### Agent: BrownMouse (Session 23)

### Deliverables

1. **New module**: `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/test_diagnostics.rs` (~530 lines)
2. **Registered** in `lib.rs` as `pub mod test_diagnostics`
3. **Three macros** exported from crate root: `diag_assert_eq!`, `diag_assert_ne!`, `diag_assert!`

### Diagnostics Contract (8 rules: D-1 through D-8)

| ID | Requirement |
|----|-------------|
| D-1 | Every test module defines `const BEAD_ID: &str` |
| D-2 | Every assertion message starts with `bead_id=` and `case=` |
| D-3 | Tests with seeds include `seed=` in failure messages |
| D-4 | Tests with fixtures include `fixture=` in failure messages |
| D-5 | Complex tests use `DiagContext` for structured assertion messages |
| D-6 | Multi-invariant tests use `DiagReport` for unified failure summaries |
| D-7 | Binary data failures include `hex_preview` of relevant bytes |
| D-8 | String comparison failures include `simple_diff` output |

### Key Types

- `DiagContext` — Fluent builder collecting bead_id, case, seed, fixture, invariant, state, hint
- `DiagReport` — Multi-finding collector with Error/Warning severity and unified rendering
- `DiagFinding` — Individual finding with context, severity, expected/actual values
- `Severity` — Error (hard failure) or Warning (soft, test continues)
- `AdoptionChecklistItem` — Reference checklist item with requirement + example code

### Utility Functions

- `simple_diff(expected, actual)` → line-based diff string
- `snapshot_kv(pairs)` → compact `{k1=v1, k2=v2}` state snapshot
- `hex_preview(data, max_bytes)` → hex dump with truncation
- `repro_command(crate, test_path)` → reproducible test re-run command

### Assertion Macros

- `diag_assert_eq!(ctx, left, right [, msg])` — equality with DiagContext on failure
- `diag_assert_ne!(ctx, left, right [, msg])` — inequality with DiagContext on failure
- `diag_assert!(ctx, cond [, msg])` — boolean with DiagContext on failure

### Tests (30 total, all passing)

- DiagContext: minimal kv, full kv, display matches format
- Diff helpers: equal returns None, different shows lines, empty vs content
- Snapshot/hex: kv formatting, empty, truncation
- Macros: pass cases, panic-with-context cases (should_panic tests)
- DiagReport: empty is ok, errors, warnings, mismatch, render, assert_ok
- Adoption checklist: count, uniqueness, examples present

### Quality Gates

- `cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib test_diagnostics`: 30/30 PASS
- `cargo fmt`: clean

-{"id":"bd-mblr.6.7","title":"Critical-path no-mock unit assertions","description":"For critical invariants identified in the coverage map, ensure at least one unit-level assertion path uses real component behavior (not trait mocks) where technically feasible. Output: auditable mapping from invariant -> non-mock unit evidence.","design":"Use invariant matrix to enforce evidence linkage and avoid ambiguous “covered somewhere” claims.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Each critical invariant has at least one non-mock unit assertion path using real component behavior where technically feasible. 2. Any unavoidable mock-only invariant must have a documented exception rationale and follow-up issue link. 3. Mapping artifact (invariant -> test evidence -> realism tier) is CI-consumable.","notes":"This task operationalizes the no-mock principle at unit granularity without forcing unrealistic test scaffolding.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:37.806164217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:47:08.793480041Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.1.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:54.478741333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:37.806164217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:54.951916876Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.7","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:01:55.245182365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1355,"issue_id":"bd-mblr.6.7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Quality intent: this bead is the explicit non-mock unit evidence guardrail. Required mapping + documented exceptions prevent silent fallback to mock-only confidence on critical invariants.","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:47:08Z"}]}

Required mapping + documented exceptions prevent silent fallback to mock-only confidence on critical invariants.","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:47:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.7","title":"Advanced Differential, Endurance, and Performance Assurance","description":"Add advanced validation capabilities that extend current coverage/no-mock/e2e/logging plan with differential semantics testing, endurance verification, performance regression governance, cross-platform durability checks, richer forensics tooling, automated bisecting, leak budgets, determinism watchdogs, and change-impact lane selection.","design":"Organize advanced assurance into nine independent but connected tracks so teams can execute in parallel while preserving a single evidence model across unit, integration, and long-run e2e validation.","acceptance_criteria":"- All child tracks bd-mblr.7.1 through bd-mblr.7.9 define explicit unit and e2e outcomes with deterministic logging requirements.
- Dependency graph remains acyclic and supports incremental adoption through clear critical paths.
- Program level reporting demonstrates reliability and performance gains without regressing concurrent writer by default behavior.","notes":"No track may disable concurrent mode defaults or reintroduce global writer serialization patterns.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:04:40.194181730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:07:51.388855960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:04:40.194181730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.1","title":"Metamorphic Differential SQL Validation","description":"Design and implement metamorphic differential testing that generates SQL programs and transformed variants, runs them against Apollo and reference SQLite, and minimizes mismatches into deterministic repro artifacts.","design":"Build a metamorphic and differential lane that mutates equivalent SQL programs and compares FrankenSQLite behavior against explicit invariants and reference semantics where overlap exists.","acceptance_criteria":"- Metamorphic transformation catalog covers parser, planner, and executor sensitive rewrites with documented invariants.
- Differential lane records deterministic seeds, replay data, and mismatch class with reducer output.
- Unit helpers and e2e scenarios are both required to isolate failures quickly before full CI sweeps.","notes":"Focus first on transaction, indexing, and concurrency visible SQL surfaces to maximize risk reduction per run minute.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:19.836445759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:07:51.463660108Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:19.836445759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-mblr.7.1.1","title":"Metamorphic workload grammar and oracle contract","description":"Define metamorphic transform families, semantic equivalence expectations, and mismatch classification rules with deterministic seed controls.","design":"Define a grammar driven workload generator and oracle contract that classify equivalence classes, tolerated differences, and forbidden divergences.","acceptance_criteria":"- Generator emits deterministic SQL corpora with configurable complexity and feature toggles.
- Oracle contract defines normalization rules for rows, ordering, null semantics, numeric tolerances, and error classes.
- Unit tests verify generator determinism and oracle classification correctness on edge cases.","notes":"Map each metamorphic rule to concrete engine invariants so future extensions remain principled.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:19.915854347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:04:57.698723908Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:04:57.698699843Z","close_reason":"Complete: TransformFamily, EquivalenceExpectation, MismatchClassification, SeedContract, OracleContract types added; all 8 transforms annotated; 453 tests passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.1.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:54.829936989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.4.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:54.907066621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-mblr.7.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:05:19.915854347Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1436,"issue_id":"bd-mblr.7.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# bd-mblr.7.1.1 — Metamorphic workload grammar and oracle contract

## Deliverables

### New types added to `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/metamorphic.rs`:
- `TransformFamily` enum: Predicate, Projection, Structural, Literal
- `EquivalenceExpectation` enum: ExactRowMatch, MultisetEquivalence, SetEquivalence, TypeCoercionEquivalent
- `MismatchClassification` enum: TrueDivergence, OrderDependentDifference, TypeAffinityDifference, NullHandlingDifference, FloatingPointDifference, FalsePositive
- `SeedContract` struct: base_seed, case_seed, entry_idx, transform_idx
- `OracleContract` struct: case_id, family, equivalence, seed
- `classify_mismatch()` function for rule-based mismatch triage
- `values_affinity_equal()` helper for SQLite type affinity comparison

### Extended existing infrastructure:
- `MetamorphicTransform` trait: added `family()` and `equivalence_expectation()` methods
- All 8 transforms updated with correct family/equivalence assignments
- `MetamorphicTestCase`: added `transform_family` and `equivalence` fields
- `MetamorphicCoverage`: added `by_transform_family` and `by_equivalence` maps
- `generate_metamorphic_corpus()`: populates new fields
- `compute_coverage()`: tracks new dimensions

## Test evidence
- 437 library unit tests passing (including ~30 new metamorphic tests)
- 16 metamorphic integration tests passing
- cargo check: pass
- cargo fmt: pass
- 0 new clippy warnings

## Reproduction
```bash
cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --lib 2>&1 | tail -5
cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test metamorphic_test 2>&1 | tail -20
```
","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:04:52Z"}]}
